channel.title: Question re: Editing others' articles
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Question re: Editing others' articles" - May we fix on-site links formatted as external links?

item.title: Moderator Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Triple-bracket links are superior to single-bracket links and should be used where possible, such as cross-links with other pages on the wiki. As such they are considered allowable formatting edits on pages.

However, it is important to be sure that they are used appropriately. Triple-bracket links with a full URL (e.g. [[[http://www.scpwiki.com/some-page]]]) are no better than single-bracket links, and some kinds of references (most notably forums) cannot be referenced using triple-bracket page name notation (i.e. [[[some-page|Link label here]]].

Provided that you are only editing pages you come across casually, I'm fine with these changes. Please do not seek out large numbers of pages to edit. If you have an extensive list of pages to be changed, please bring it to staff instead.

Also note that staff reserves the right to issue cease-and-desist orders for any edits being carried out in an improper manner.

item.title: Question re: Editing others' articles
item.content:encoded:
Hi, I hope I've found the right place for site rules questions. My question's about this rule (emphasis mine):

Editing others' articles: You may correct grammar, spelling, or formatting errors on other peoples' articles. Please put a summary of changes in the 'description of changes' box. Any further edits require permission from the original author or Rewrite team.

Often, an on-site link (triple brackets) is formatted as an external link (single brackets). As the Google Chrome users among you may already know, these external links don't work for some users while they are logged in (because we visit scp-wiki.wikidot.com instead of scpwiki.com as a login workaround; visiting any link formatted as https://scpwiki.com/whatever gives us an error message1). Clicking a link that ought to be an on-site link and receiving an error is unpleasant, as is manually replacing scpwiki with scp-wiki.wikidot in the address bar.

I don't know if using the 'wrong' link type is technically a formatting error, but I am hoping to receive mod/admin confirmation that it falls under this category.2 Changing these links' format type would not alter the article text, it would just make the links work properly for those of us who have to use scp-wiki.wikidot.com instead of scpwiki.com for technical reasons.

Getting author permission each and every time, just to change link formatting type from external to on-site, would be hugely annoying for both the editor and the author. I am hoping that we readers can be allowed to make these formatting changes without obtaining author permission each time.

So, tl;dr: If a link is formatted as an external link, but would work properly as an on-site link, may we go ahead and make that formatting change without bothering the author for their permission?

Footnotes
1. Note that the error only appears when an external link specifies https. It's some sort of certificate recognition error. Links formatted as on-site links work fine for us Chrome users, because wikidot helpfully inserts whichever base address the reader is already using.
2. I actually created an account because I wanted to fix various minor errors as I came across them while reading.
